description_long: This object is a fresh ginger rhizome, characterized by its irregular, knobby, and bulbous shape with multiple protruding sections. The surface is covered in a thin, pale yellow to light tan skin, with visible ridges and faint lines marking the boundaries between segments. The texture appears rough and slightly fibrous, with some areas showing minor scarring or natural blemishes typical of ginger roots. The overall form is compact, with several rounded nodes branching from a central body, giving it a hand-like appearance. The coloration is consistent with fresh ginger, showing subtle variations in hue and a matte, non-glossy finish. This ginger root would be commonly found in markets, grocery stores, or as discarded organic matter in urban environments.
description: A knobby, pale yellow ginger root with a rough, fibrous surface and multiple rounded protrusions. Its irregular, segmented form and matte finish are typical of fresh ginger rhizomes.
description_view_0: The ginger root is seen from an angle highlighting its largest bulbous section and two prominent side nodes, with visible surface ridges and a rough texture.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the rounded end of the ginger, showing a cleanly cut section and a more elongated body with subtle surface lines.
description_view_2: The ginger is viewed from the opposite side, displaying a broad, rounded node and a more compact arrangement of the protrusions.
description_view_3: From this angle, the ginger's three main nodes are clearly visible, radiating from the central body and showing the most pronounced segmentation.
